headline: Orang Asli NCR land claims backlog exceeds 200 cases
context: Over 200 native customary rights land claims by Orang Asli communities remain unresolved in Malaysian courts as of 2025.

cards.big: 200+ Orang Asli land rights cases pending in courts spanning 15 years
cards.sub: Communities lose ancestral land to logging and plantation development

cards.big: Aboriginal Peoples Act 1954 classifies Orang Asli as wards of the state
cards.sub: No statutory recognition of native customary land rights for Peninsular Malaysia

cards.big: Federal Court in Sagong Tasi 2002 recognised Orang Asli common law rights
cards.sub: Landmark ruling not consistently applied across lower courts 23 years later

cards.big: Over 30,000 Orang Asli affected by land dispossession since 2010
cards.sub: Deforestation of ancestral territories linked to poverty rate of 33.6%

cards.big: When courts take 15 years, justice becomes dispossession by delay
JAKOA gazetted only 18,000 hectares of Orang Asli reserves since 2015
